hooks.why: Reads/derives the disposition-ledger sidecar under audit/, the symlinked shared store every checkout reaches through main; anchors on gaia_resolve_main_root so the check reads the one real copy regardless of which tree the merge command runs from.
Touches only the tracked .gaia/VERSION file and a per-worktree git-dir stamp lock (git's own --absolute-git-dir, correct by construction); no .gaia/local root to resolve.
Pattern-matches a Bash command string only; touches no tree-scoped state.
Pattern-matches a Bash command string only; touches no tree-scoped state.
Pattern-matches an Edit/Write file_path only; touches no tree-scoped state.
Pattern-matches an Edit/Write file_path only; touches no tree-scoped state.
Validates the YAML content of an Edit/Write payload only; touches no tree-scoped state.
Pattern-matches an Edit/Write file_path only; touches no tree-scoped state.

Reads the setup-in-progress standdown sentinel, a per-machine marker anchored to main; a resolver failure falls back to the prior process-cwd derivation rather than allowing, since this guard is fail-closed on ambiguity, never fail-open.
Pattern-matches an Edit/Write file_path against .gaia/manifest.json only; touches no .gaia/local state.
Pattern-matches a Bash command string only; touches no tree-scoped state.

Already resolver-based: reads the registry's rm_whitelist (gaia_registry_rm_whitelist, itself main-anchored through state-registry-lib.sh) to carve safe-scratch directories out of its absolute-path rm -rf deny. The whitelist is a distinct top-level registry list with no entry ids of its own, so its repo-relative targets are declared as path: tokens. Declared here, not converted (already correct).
Pattern-matches an Edit/Write payload for secret-shaped content only; touches no tree-scoped state.

Resolves the repo toplevel only to relativize a candidate path for its refusal-set match (test/, .claude/, the CI pipeline, root build config); the refusal set names no .gaia/local path (.gaia/local is deliberately exempt, per lib/audit-selfheal-paths.sh's own header).
Reads/writes the spec-to-plan chain guard sentinel under cache/, deliberately resolved to main so the guard holds across a mid-session tree switch; anchors on gaia_resolve_main_root.
Pattern-matches an Edit/Write payload against tsconfig content only; touches no tree-scoped state.

Already resolver-based: compares the acting tree's root against gaia_resolve_main_root and reads the registry's shared + main-only-dir lists (gaia_registry_linkable_paths / gaia_registry_main_only_dirs) to exempt every main-anchored write path from its wrong-checkout deny. Declared here, not converted (already correct).
Delegates its cache path and write entirely to the already resolver-based .gaia/scripts/gh-artifact-lib.sh; this hook itself derives no root and holds no bare .gaia/local literal.

Appends genuinely-failing test observations to the RED ledger for the tree where the gated Bash action ran; anchors on gaia_resolve_tree_root, applied to the payload cwd (falling back to process cwd) since the acting tree is the one question that matters for this state.

Writes a once-per-session advisory marker at a fixed repo-relative path in whatever checkout the session runs in; a tracked-working-file marker, not .gaia/local state, so no root to resolve (Pattern D: declared, no conversion).
Pattern-matches a component index.tsx file_path against a sibling stories file only; touches no tree-scoped state.

Arms main's shared debt-count staleness sentinel after a debt-mutating gh command; anchors on gaia_resolve_main_root so a worktree fix arms the one cache the statusline actually reads, closing a prior latent wrong-tree write.
Reads and re-arms main's shared debt-count cache/sentinel at session start; anchors on gaia_resolve_main_root, same reasoning as debt-sentinel-touch.sh.
Checks the tracked .gaia/manifest.json and the built maintainer CLI binary only; touches no .gaia/local state.

Already reads the state registry (main-anchored internally via state-registry-lib.sh) for its generic residue sweep; its own root variable is the CURRENT tree (git rev-parse --show-toplevel), with a separate hand-derived main-root lookup for its worktree-teardown sub-sweep only. Declared here, not converted (already reviewed and frozen).

Posts the GAIA-Audit commit status only after every dispatched member's clearance marker (main-anchored shared state) is confirmed; anchors on gaia_resolve_main_root. Agent-invoked, not settings-registered, but it derives a root so it still declares here.

Resolves a PR number and an incremental audit base, then delegates the findings-block post entirely to .gaia/scripts/post-findings-block.sh; this hook itself derives no root and holds no .gaia/local literal.

Reads every dispatched Code Audit Team member's clearance marker under audit/, the symlinked shared store; anchors on gaia_resolve_main_root so the gate reads the one real copy regardless of which tree the merge command runs from.

Reads the RED ledger for the tree where the gated git commit runs, requiring a matching failing-run record for a new-at-HEAD passing test; anchors on gaia_resolve_tree_root, applied to the payload cwd (falling back to process cwd).
Reads/writes the block-once escape state under cache/shared/serena-guard/, machine-scoped shared state; anchors on gaia_resolve_main_root rather than the current tree's symlinked copy.

Resolves the active plan folder (main-anchored, via lib/gaia-active-plan.sh) and the cost ledger (main-anchored, via the already resolver-based .gaia/scripts/ledger-path-lib.sh) to render the merge-time cost roll-up. No local derivation of its own; inherits both anchors (verified, not converted).

Cheap-gates on a live plan RUNNING sentinel (main-anchored) before invoking token-tally.sh, which appends to the shared cost ledger; sources the resolver directly for its own cheap-gate main_root, and inherits the same anchor via lib/gaia-active-plan.sh for plan resolution.

Resolves the active plan folder (main-anchored, via lib/gaia-active-plan.sh) to key a review-cost tally row appended to the shared cost ledger. No local derivation of its own; inherits the anchor (verified, not converted).

Reads the tracked wiki/.state.json (identical content in every checkout via git) to compute a drift count; [ -d .git ]-dead surface owned by a later phase's conversion, but it writes no per-checkout marker of its own today.

Writes a once-per-session drift-reminder marker at a fixed repo-relative path in whatever checkout the session runs in; a tracked-working-file marker, not .gaia/local state. [ -d .git ]-dead surface; a later phase owns any future conversion.

Reads wiki/hot.md and consumes the .claude/wiki-recompact-pending sentinel, both tracked/working files at fixed repo-relative paths in whatever checkout the prompt is submitted in; no .gaia/local root to resolve (Pattern D: declared, no conversion; the payload cwd is not even measured on UserPromptSubmit).

Writes the .claude/wiki-recompact-pending sentinel after confirming wiki/hot.md exists, both tracked/working files at fixed repo-relative paths; no .gaia/local root to resolve (Pattern D: declared, no conversion; the payload cwd is not even measured on PostCompact).
Records HEAD under the per-worktree git-dir (git rev-parse --git-dir, correct by construction, oracle G) and delegates to the already-converted local-janitor.sh; touches no .gaia/local state itself.

Writes the .claude/wiki-safety-checked once-per-session marker at a fixed repo-relative path, alongside a per-worktree git-dir session marker (oracle G, correct); [ -d .git ]-dead surface, a later phase owns any future conversion.
Manipulates git history (reset/commit/push/PR) only; writes no per-checkout marker and touches no .gaia/local state. [ -d .git ]-dead surface owned by a later phase's conversion.

Reads the per-tree worthiness ledger for the tree where the gated gh pr merge runs; anchors on gaia_resolve_tree_root, applied to the payload cwd (falling back to process cwd) since the acting tree is the one question that matters for this state.
Every function takes <root> as a parameter from its caller; this lib derives no root of its own.
Every function takes <root> as a parameter from its caller; this lib derives no root of its own.
Every function takes a sidecar path as a parameter from its caller; this lib derives no root of its own.
A pure path-list matcher with no root parameter and no filesystem access at all.
Every function takes <root> as a parameter from its caller; this lib derives no root of its own.
Exports one ERE constant; no filesystem access, no root derivation.

Resolves the active plan folder's RUNNING sentinel, which lives only in the main checkout (.gaia/local/plans, .gaia/local/specs); resolve_active_plan_dir sources the shared resolver deferred into its own body and calls gaia_resolve_main_root, replacing a hand-rolled twin of the same derivation (deleted in the same change).
Reads .gaia/automation.json, a tracked repo-relative config file, not .gaia/local state; touches no tree-scoped state.

red_ledger_path takes a root argument (the caller's payload-anchored tree_root); when none is supplied it defaults to gaia_resolve_tree_root of the process cwd, sourcing the resolver deferred into its own body so the lib's no-side-effects-at-source-time contract holds.
Compares the home repo's git toplevel against a command's target repo for cross-repo scoping; touches no .gaia/local state.
